equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: Ethereum add-ons register Ritual RPC methods but do not construct or spawn an AsyncDetectionService background task.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing construction of AsyncDetectionService from node provider and pool and missing task_executor spawn of async_detection_service.start().
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ethereum/node/src/node.rs launch_add_ons clones provider/pool to build ritual_async_rpc::module and merges RPC modules.
The same launch_add_ons code uses ctx.node.task_executor() for ValidationApi but does not spawn an async detection service.
Async detection exists inline in payload/RPC validation paths, but not as the background service required by this invariant.

rationale: The primary diff adds ritual-async as an Ethereum node dependency, imports AsyncDetectionService, builds it from ctx.node.provider() and ctx.node.pool(), and spawns async_detection_service.start() on the node task executor with startup/error logging. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When the Ethereum node add-ons are launched, the node must construct and spawn an AsyncDetectionService with the node provider and transaction pool so async transaction detection runs in the background during node operation.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run the Ethereum node launch path with a test provider/pool and task executor that records spawned futures, then assert exactly one AsyncDetectionService start task is spawned during add-on launch and receives the node provider and pool; the test should fail if node startup no longer starts detection.
summary: Ethereum node add-ons do not launch the requested async detection service.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instrument EthereumAddOns::launch_add_ons with a recording task executor; current local code should spawn no AsyncDetectionService task.
title: Ethereum node launches async detection
